questions.prompt: What does findElements (plural) return when nothing on the page matches the locator?
options: It returns the closest non-matching element
questions.explanation: findElements returns a List of all matches and an empty list when there are none — it never throws, which makes it the safe choice when absence is allowed.

questions.prompt: Which exception does findElement (singular) throw when no element matches?
questions.explanation: findElement returns the first match or throws NoSuchElementException when there are zero matches.

questions.prompt: Why is a long absolute XPath like /html/body/div[2]/form/input[1] a poor choice?
options: XPath is not supported by ChromeDriver
It is brittle and breaks as soon as the page structure changes
It can only match the last element on the page
It is slower than downloading the whole DOM
questions.explanation: Absolute paths depend on the exact nesting of the page, so any layout change breaks them. Prefer a stable id or a concise CSS selector.

questions.prompt: You want to count how many links are on a page and act only if there are any. Which approach fits best?
options: findElements(By.tagName("a")) and check the list size
driver.get() the page twice to be sure
questions.explanation: findElements returns every match as a List, so you can check size() or isEmpty() without relying on exception handling for normal control flow.
